text: Let n be an odd number greater than 100. Consider a binary minheap with n elements stored in an array P whose index starts from 1. Which of the following indices of P does NOT correspond to any leaf node of the minheap?

Consider a hash table P[0..10] initially empty, using open addressing with linear probing. Hash function h(x) = (x+7) mod 11. Insert: 1, 13, 22, 15, 11, 24. Which positions are empty after these insertions?
The height of a binary tree is the number of edges in the longest path from root to leaf. The maximum possible height of a full binary tree with 23 nodes is ______. (answer in integer)
Consider the C code that computes number of nodes in a non-empty singly linked list: int getListSize(struct node *head) { if(E1) return 1; return E2; } Which replacements for E1 and E2 are correct?
Let P be the set of integers 1 to 15. Consider any order of insertion of elements of P into a BST that creates a complete binary tree. Which element can NEVER be the third element inserted?
Let G be an undirected graph which is a path on 8 vertices. The number of matchings in G is ______. (answer in integer)

The following sequence is the preorder traversal of a BST T: 50, 25, 13, 40, 30, 47, 75, 60, 70, 80, 77. The position of element 60 in the postorder traversal of T is ______. (answer in integer, position begins with 1)
